nodes.title: Normalize receiver and length first
nodes.why: This makes the method work on array-like values and ensures length is treated as a safe integer.
nodes.alternative: Use `this` directly and trust it is a clean array.
nodes.tradeoff: More spec-aligned and defensive, with slightly more setup code.

nodes.title: Handle initial accumulator explicitly
nodes.why: `reduce` behaves differently depending on whether `initialValue` is passed, so this branch is critical.
nodes.alternative: Always require `initialValue` and skip this logic.
nodes.tradeoff: Matches native behavior, but adds branching complexity.

nodes.title: Skip sparse indexes while reducing
nodes.why: Native `reduce` ignores holes in sparse arrays; this check preserves that behavior.
nodes.alternative: Iterate every numeric index and treat missing values as `undefined`.
nodes.tradeoff: Correct sparse-array semantics, with an extra property check on each step.

nodes.title: Coerce length in a spec-leaning way
nodes.why: This normalizes odd length values and prevents negative or non-numeric lengths from breaking traversal.
nodes.alternative: Use `>>> 0` for a shorter uint32 coercion.
nodes.tradeoff: More explicit intent, but longer expression.

nodes.title: Use `k in O` to find first present slot
nodes.why: This walks past holes until a real element is found, matching reduce’s sparse-array behavior.
nodes.tradeoff: Concise sparse handling, though `in` may include inherited numeric keys in unusual cases.

nodes.title: Call callback only for present indexes

anchor.snippet: if (k in O) acc = callbackFn(acc, O[k], k, O);

nodes.why: This keeps callback calls aligned with how native reduce skips missing entries.
nodes.alternative: Call callback on all indexes and pass `undefined` for holes.
nodes.tradeoff: Closer native parity, with a guard on each loop iteration.

nodes.title: Extract hole-skipping into a helper
nodes.why: Moving this logic to one helper reduces repetition and makes traversal intent easier to read.
nodes.alternative: Inline the skip loop in every place it is needed.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner structure, at the cost of one extra helper function.

nodes.title: Reuse helper for initial-value branch
nodes.why: Both branches need the first present index, so helper reuse keeps behavior consistent.
nodes.alternative: Maintain separate loops for each branch.
nodes.tradeoff: Less duplicated code, but slightly more indirection when reading.

nodes.title: Advance loop through present indexes only

anchor.snippet: for (k = nextPresentIndex(k); k < len; k = nextPresentIndex(k + 1)) {

nodes.why: Loop stepping directly to the next valid slot avoids repeated hole checks inside the body.
nodes.alternative: Increment by one and guard with `hasOwnProperty` each iteration.
nodes.tradeoff: Readable high-level flow, though the loop header is more advanced.
